name: Tsukahara Full Vault
description: Round off onto the vault and off into same-side full twist. For example, right-side round off into a right (clock-wise) twist. In Gymnastics, this is called Kasamastsu Vault where as Tsukahara Full Vault twists counter-momentum to the Round Off. This was first landed by Shigeru Kasamatsu, the 1974 world gymnastics all-around champion gold medalist.

name: Pimp Flip
description: A back tuck parallel to an obstacle set by pushing down on top of the obstacle with one hand. In general, a Pimp is any flip with a back tuck while pushing off the top of an obstacle. This was first landed by Owen Hegarty in 2008. According to Owen Hegarty, during his first attempt at the move in the gym, Jenx remarked, "That's pimpin, mate." At the time, there was debate over whether to call it the "Monkey Gainer" or "Kong Gainer," so as a joke, the group started referring to it as the "Hegarty Big Pimpin Flip." Eventually, Hegarty shortened the name to "Pimp Flip."

name: Palm Walk
description: Jump parallel to the wall, placing your nearest hand on it. Shift your hips forward and toward the wall while turning your body away from it. Lift your closest leg over your hips, followed by the outside leg. As your hips rise above head level, rotate your chest away from the wall and place your outside hand underneath, using it to palm the wall for support. Let both legs continue to circle across the wall while repositioning your first hand in front of the outside one as you bring your chest down to prepare for landing. This move was first landed by Soroush Bahrami in 2021.

name: Raiz
description: Raiz is a Capoeira-derived acrobatic skill characterized by a backward-leaning, arched takeoff from one leg, chest open, and hips driven upward, producing an off-axis, inverted aerial-like motion without the hands touching the ground. There are two common variations: Low-fast Raiz uses a more compressed, Gumbi-like mechanics with no hands, often described as an inverted Aerial. High-floaty Raiz, more common in parkour, uses a tornado-kick-style takeoff into a laid-back arch during the flight phase, emphasizing height and air-time, and a pronounced off-axis inversion.

name: Sit Turn
description: Sit Turn begins similar to Lazy Vault, using the inside leg (the one closest to the hand initially placed on the rail). Once that leg clears the wall, lean forward and lift your opposite leg, following the path of the first. As you spin on the rail, sit down and place your opposite hand on it for balance. Use the spin's momentum to swing your first leg back to its starting side, with the second leg following closely behind.
